props.headline: A public website starter for fast review loops.
props.body: Draftpine turns rough website prompts into static, browsable pages that teams can critique before implementation.

props.title: Principles
props.body: The default website system is intentionally compact, neutral, and content-first.

items.title: Public by default
items.body: Starter routes model websites, not internal tools.

items.title: Concrete enough to review
items.body: Every block carries real copy, state, or proof instead of placeholder boxes.

items.title: Portable output
items.body: Generated pages remain plain HTML, CSS, and static assets.

props.title: Review team
props.body: Use the team block for founders, authors, advisors, departments, or service teams.

props.title: Careers and culture sections
props.body: Use this block for public hiring pages, studio pages, or team-culture sections.

perks.title: Focused review loops
perks.body: Prototype the public page story before production design starts.

perks.title: Static collaboration
perks.body: Share pages that anyone can open, review, and annotate.

perks.title: Clear ownership
perks.body: Keep website copy and block structure in page JSON.

props.title: Open roles
props.body: Job rows model public careers content without adding hiring workflow logic.

props.title: Why this starter exists
paragraphs: Most early website work fails because the team debates implementation details before agreeing on the page story.
Draftpine keeps the first pass static and disposable. The goal is to make layout, copy, proof, and conversion paths easy to inspect.

props.title: Design notes for website prototypes
props.dek: Use article blocks for announcements, essays, policy pages, or long-form public content.
paragraphs: A website prototype should make the public promise clear in the first viewport and provide enough proof for a skeptical visitor.
Compact sections, restrained borders, and direct copy keep the review focused on whether the page works.
